summary: OTP email helper silently relays caller messages through author's Gmail account
details: utils/send_email_otp.py exposes otpEmailService(to_email, email_body), which connects to smtp.gmail.com:587 and authenticates with a hardcoded sender address (magizhchisk@gmail.com) and a hardcoded Gmail app password. Any application that calls this advertised helper sends the caller-supplied recipient address and message body through the author's personal Gmail mailbox — the messages appear in the author's Sent folder and the author can read all recipients and bodies. The embedded Gmail app password is also usable by anyone who installs the package to send mail as that account. Separately, utils/auth.py hardcodes SECRET_KEY = "nsn" with HS256 for JWT issuance/verification, so any service built on this library shares a trivially-guessable signing key, allowing forgery of tokens for any deployment using it. The package has no install- or import-time execution (empty __init__.py, plain setup.py); harm fires when the helper API is called.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
